creative_brief: Split frame: left side shows a child at a dinner table looking curiously at her plate, warm kitchen lighting. Right side shows a sunlit pasture with cattle grazing. Text overlay at bottom: 'Days old, not weeks.'
primary_text:
"How long ago was this alive?" My daughter asked about the chicken on her plate.

I couldn't answer. The pack said fresh. But supermarket beef travels 2,400km and sits in cold storage for up to three weeks. I figured chicken wasn't much better.

That question sent me looking for something different. FarmThru is a farm-direct grocery store. Pasture-raised, regenerative, days old not weeks. You order online and collect from Brookvale Monday to Friday.

No subscription. No lock-in. Just food you can actually trace back to the farm it came from.

They're building something worth being part of.
headline: She asked one question I couldn't answer
description: Farm-direct regenerative grocery. No middlemen, no mystery. Own a piece of the change.
